title: sign_verify_roundtrip test is its own oracle
description: Trigger: key.sign(hash) and key.verify(hash, sig) use the same EccKey on the same TPM. Bad behavior: if both sign and verify are consistently mis-implemented (e.g. wrong hash algorithm, wrong curve), the test still passes — it proves round-trip self-consistency, not correctness. An interop bug (signatures not verifiable by openssl/RustCrypto) would not be caught. Per the project test integrity rule: 'Never write a test that uses the code under test as its own oracle.' Fix: add a test that verifies a hard-coded externally-generated ECDSA P-256/SHA-256 signature against a known key — OR export the TPM public key and verify the produced signature with p256/ecdsa from RustCrypto as an independent oracle.
